home.image_card_desc: Browse and manage local image resources
home.ice_ascendancy_text: The blizzard's howl drowns out all cries. Where frost descends, all clamor falls to silence. The struggles and mediocrity of this world—I have never deigned to notice.
home.ice_quote_1: My exquisite Frostfinery robe will be ready tomorrow. The tailor says the ice-thread embroidery is simply gorgeous. Once I put it on, I absolutely must take a stroll through Snowfall Court and let those country bumpkins feast their eyes.
home.ice_quote_2: Frozen solid and still won't shut up—the racket is giving me a headache. If you can't learn to keep quiet, then stay silent forever.
home.ice_quote_3: Who drew this dungeon map? They can't even mark the roads right. Forget it, I'm done searching—I'll just clear the whole place. Leave nothing standing.
home.ice_quote_4: (Flips through quest map) Today's legendary quest... the temple of the "Queen of the North"? She dares claim the title? The only queen this world shall ever know is me. That barrel waist of hers—ha. Still, I hear she holds a decent-quality Blueflame crystal. And I just happen to be missing a brooch—she'd better hand it over willingly, or else... hmf. I'll annihilate her and tear down her pathetic little temple.
home.ice_quote_5: Scored a pair of ice-blue earrings today—lovely quality. Grabbed a Snowcap milk tea from the shop next door while I was at it. That fool of a shopkeeper finally remembered extra sugar, light ice. If he weren't so easy on the eyes... hm, I'd have switched shops long ago.
home.ice_quote_7: Tomorrow I'll head to Magma City. Heard someone scored a top-grade Elemental Fusion Crystal. I'll swing by and check on my bestie while I'm there—heard she's been working on some new flame spell. Whatever, give her the opportunity to treat me to dinner. Mm, it's settled.
home.ice_quote_8: Been following this drama. Everyone in it is dumb as a pig—makes me want to freeze them all into ice sculptures and put them in a display case. I'd call it 'The Pig Ice Exhibit.'
home.ice_quote_9: Aside from this one that's barely passable, the rest is all garbage. What a shabby, miserable dump. I'm never coming back.
home.ice_quote_10: Frost Burial: Realm of Eternal Silence. It is an honor that I would use this technique on you. Now... quietly fade away.
home.ice_quote_11: Stared at the Frostmoon Mirror for ages. This face really is... flawless. Tch. Looking good in absolutely everything—what a bother.
home.ice_quote_12: I heard that young master from Snowfall Keep sent some so-called 'rare gift' through a messenger again. Hah. Tried to hit on Elena the other day, what a total scumbag. Rejected him countless times and still he clings on shamelessly. So pathetic. To court me, he would need to be the continent's mightiest warrior. Someone like... Adrian of the Northern Blade. Now that is a man worthy of me. As for that castle lordling—he can go cool off somewhere far, far away.
home.ice_quote_13: Elena, watch out! (A wall of frost rises in an instant, blocking the monster's lethal blow) BOOM—tch. So you're not the type to shatter at a single touch. But that just... makes this slightly more entertaining.
home.ice_quote_14: Just die already! Elena is waiting for me in ranked! I'm hitting King today—I need to hit King! (sobs)
home.ice_quote_15: Another ten-pull, another off-banner. Does this gacha even have a pity system? Should I file a complaint to consumer protection?
home.ice_quote_16: Another one? No need to introduce yourself—your eyes already gave you away. Don't overthink it, or else... (frost trails gently across the screen's edge) I wouldn't mind letting you feel just how close ice and fire can be.
home.ice_quote_17: Boss, I'm your new secretary Eira Frost. Did you rest well last night? Leave everything to me.
home.cg_scene1_text: Nova City — 2079. A world of uncertainty — order and chaos coexist, the underground darknet, block councils, and the Order Patrol hold each other in check. Neon signs light every alley, and everyone writes their own rules. I'm Aya, Order Patrol Squad 7 captain. Today's my solo patrol shift.
home.cg_scene2_text: Music, shows, art, games — my happy formula. After every mission, the captain shakes his head and says "Aya, charging in without waiting for orders again." I pull off my tactical gloves, dust them off. Silver Spirit, sync tactical link.
home.cg_scene3_text: But one person can only do so much. What really matters — fighting alongside my squad, keeping this city safe. Argent, sync tactical link.
home.cg_scene4_text: (Encrypted comm crackles) Aya, abnormal signals detected near your location in the Old Quarter, Sector 7. Unregistered cyber mercenaries massing — military-grade. Patrol Command orders on-site recon. Await backup.
home.cg_scene5_text: Tch, quite a crowd today. Been on a losing streak in games lately — perfect timing to blow off some steam.
home.cg_scene6_text: (Tactical earpiece crackles again) "Aya, don't do it! Too many hostiles — backup squad is almost there!" Sorry, Command. Sequence already executed — cannot abort. (Command: Dammit, where's that backup squad? Tell them to hurry...) (Presses chest core, violet light bursting through her fingers) Limiter release protocol initiated. Once started, there's no turning back. Sync rate climbing — warning: local magnetic field distorting.
home.cg_scene7_text: 80% energy limiter released! Core temperature stable. 「EMP Field」ability online — enemy electronics within 100m radius experiencing interference. Mercenary comm links crackling with static and lag.
home.cg_scene8_text: 90% energy limiter released! 「Data Storm」ability online — battlefield data flooding the HUD. Every enemy position, equipment model, and weak point tagged in real time. Argent materializes from the data stream, crouching at my feet — pupils locked on the enemy, ready to strike.
home.cg_scene9_text: 100% — all energy limiters released. Core resonance at critical threshold, sync rate exceeding 137%! Argent, activate flight armor protocol! (Argent leaps — armor unfolds from its spine, metallic wings gleaming cold light through the neon haze. Transformation complete.)
home.cg_scene10_text: Argent, full power output! EMP — discharge! (Ring-shaped EMP shockwave sweeps the battlefield) Energy lightning crashing from above, Argent's laser beam piercing through enemy ranks — mercenary gear exploding one after another. Let this scrap heap witness the true Code Phantom!
home.cg_scene11_text: (Smoke slowly clears, battlefield falls silent) All targets eliminated. The mercenaries' bodies dissolve into digital ash, drifting away in the neon night. Nothing remains but charred weapon wreckage and scattered cybernetic parts. Argent flicks its tail, purring with satisfaction.
home.cg_scene12_text:
(The rumble of engines in the distance — Squad 7's armored transport finally arrives.)
"Aya! You went solo again—" The captain jumps out, stops mid-sentence at the sight of the wreckage, and sighs. "...Fine. At least you're alive."
(Aya wipes the ash from her face with her sleeve. She wants to laugh, but coughs twice instead.)
"Alive, and we won. But—"
(She crouches down, picks up a charred piece of equipment, and flips it over to reveal the nameplate on the back.)
"Look at this. Military-grade comm module, encrypted tactical channel — standard-issue gear. But their coordination was practically nonexistent. Top-shelf equipment, bottom-tier fighting — they weren't here to win. These were scouts. Someone sent them to test our firepower."
home.cg_scene13_text:
(The tactical earpiece erupts with a flood of emergency transmissions—)
"Command Center bulletin: Squad 7, be advised — multiple hostile signals emerging simultaneously across the Old Quarter. Sector 3 Street, Dock Warehouse District, Abandoned Subway Entrance, North Outpost Perimeter — at least four locations, all registering cyber mercenary combat formations. Repeat, this is not an isolated attack."
(The captain's face darkens.)
"Multiple flashpoints across the Old Quarter, all at once — this isn't probing. This is a coordinated offensive."
home.cg_scene14_text:
"But why the Old Quarter?" Aya rises, staring into the distance. "Transient population — mercenaries blend right in. Maze-like architecture, underground warrens in every direction — urban warfare puts us at a disadvantage everywhere. And besides—"
(She stops cold.)
"Abandoned Subway Entrance... North Outpost..." Aya's eyes widen. "The North Outpost sits right on top of the Old Quarter's underground conduit hub. The subway's construction tunnels from before it was abandoned run directly above those conduits. If they breach both points—"
Argent's ears shoot up, a low growl rumbling in its throat.
"—they'll have a direct path into the Central Energy Hub's main transfer lines."
home.cg_scene15_text:
(A brief silence falls over the comm channel, then Command's voice returns, urgent and sharp.)
"Aya's assessment has been integrated into the tactical picture. All units, listen up — if the Central Energy Hub falls, Nova City goes dark. A probing vanguard to map our defenses, followed by a multi-pronged feint to scatter our patrols — this is no impulsive strike. This is war, planned and premeditated."
"Command is authorizing deployment of Titan-class Sentinel Mechs. Squad 7, split up and hold every underground access point. Do not let the enemy anywhere near the Central Energy Hub."
home.cg_scene16_text:
(Aya presses her hand against her chest core. Argent lets out a low battle-howl at her feet.)
"So many hostiles surfacing across the Old Quarter at the exact same moment — there's no way that's coincidence. But who's behind all of this... and what are they really after?"
(In the distance, beneath the neon glow, a colossal Sentinel Mech rises — its engines shaking the entire street.)
The real fight is only beginning.
